name: SuggestionList
description: Vertically-stacked block of follow suggestions rendered as a swipeable pager. One page shows three rows; the next page peeks at the trailing edge to invite the swipe. Each row pairs a Thumbnail at the 48 rung with a stacked text column (display name, follower / meta count, description) and a trailing Toggle Button that flips between 'Follow' and 'Following'. Anatomy is entity-agnostic — suggested channels, suggested people, suggested companies, suggested topics all share the same shape.

headerAction.description: { label, href? , onClick? } — trailing text link in the header.

items.description: Array of channel descriptors: { value, name, followers, description, thumbnail, active?, onToggle? }.

container.description: Surface block holding header above the swipeable pager.

header.description: Single row with section label leading and optional text-link action trailing. Stays anchored while pages scroll.

headerAction.description: Trailing [Text Button](../button/text.md) (`size={'xsmall'}`, `appearance={'accent'}`) — typically 'See all'. Renders as a link-affordance, so it follows the family-wide rule: link-shaped Text Buttons take `accent` for chromatic emphasis (see [text.md](../button/text.md#accent) and rule 8 of the agent contract).

pager.description: Horizontally-scrolling track of pages with mandatory inline scroll-snap; native scrollbar hidden.

page.description: Vertical stack of exactly three rows. Width = container inner width minus a peek amount.

row.description: Single channel suggestion. Flex row: avatar → text column → trailing toggle.

avatar.description: Thumbnail at size 48 — every prop forwarded verbatim.

textColumn.description: Vertical stack of channelName, followers, description. min-width: 0 so children can truncate.

trailingAction.description: Toggle Button — the row's only commit affordance. The row itself is not clickable.

sizing.headerActionRendersAs: Button variant='text' size='xsmall' appearance='accent' — label paints in sys.color.primary via the Text Button accent token.

thumbnail.description: Forwarded to Thumbnail verbatim — src, alt, updateDot, logoBadge. Agents MUST pass `src`; fill `/placeholder.png` when no real subject is implied. `fallbackOnMissingSrc` is the runtime safety net for load failures, not a scaffold-time omission license.

states.note: Container has no interactive state. Each row's only interactive surface is the trailing Toggle Button (its own state contract). The headerAction is a text link (Text links state contract). The row body is presentational — tapping does not route.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Channel List's own rows are presentational (tap-to-route is not owned by the row body); this contract documents the composition any nested focus target inherits — the trailing Toggle Button paints its own outward ring per its spec, but any forthcoming row-level focus target tiles flush with hairline dividers and would use the Inward composition.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: Rows tile the column with a hairline divider; an outward ring would overlap divider and neighbour row.

ring.outerLayerPosition: depth 0..2px from the row edge (the outer stroke)
ring.insetLayerPosition: depth 2..3px from the row edge (the counter-ring just inside the outer stroke)
ring.implementation: inset box-shadow constrained strictly inside the row's footprint.

focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

behavior.pagesOfThree: A page is always exactly three rows. If items is not a multiple of three, the final page is padded with empty space — it never collapses to a shorter page.
behavior.horizontalScrollSnap: scroll-snap-type: x mandatory; each page declares scroll-snap-align: start.
behavior.nextPagePeek: Pager bleeds out by container.md (the container's right padding) via negative margin-right so the peek isn't clipped. Page basis composes the inter-page gap (inline.xl) plus the visible peek (inline.md, 8px) into one calc — the 8px peek holds across the responsive breakpoint.
behavior.toggleCommitsInPlace: Tapping 'Follow' flips the row's button to 'Following' and stays there. State is owned by the consumer via items[i].active + onToggle.

forbidden: row leading thumbnail rendered outside the chorus-thumbnail slot
follow action rendered as button/standard — the follow affordance is button/toggle per the spec
pager-style scroll snap removed — the swipeable pager is the family contract
more than the declared page-of-rows shown at once — overflow is the swipeable affordance, not a wrap
